title: Can you get a signals width from the c++ harness
body:
Hi!

I am using Verilator to build an emulator for a processor. I am emulating an external memory with a simple c array. I need to allocate memory for this array. I would like to width of the address signal for memory allocation, as the processor can be configured for different size external memories. I was wondering if there is a get this from the verilator generated code? something like

```cpp
int addr_width = c->io_SRamCtrl_ramOut_addr.width();
```
I thought that there is a good chance since i can see the size from the generated .h file:
VL_OUT(io_SRamCtrl_ramOut_addr,**19**,0);
I appreciate the help - Thank you!
